purpose.paragraphTwoEmphasis: lives through compassion and collective effort.

story.title: The Story of Alokayon
story.intro: What began as a small act of compassion has grown into a structured movement of dignity-centered support for vulnerable communities across Bangladesh.

sections.title: A Beginning Rooted in Compassion
paragraphs: Alokayon was born from a simple but powerful realization: even as Bangladesh has progressed over the decades, countless people still live without access to basic necessities such as food, shelter, education, and healthcare.

Behind growth statistics are real lives — widows struggling in silence, orphaned children unable to continue their education, elderly individuals without support, and families living in extreme hardship. Many of them, bound by dignity and circumstance, cannot even ask for help.

In 2011, with this reality in mind, a small initiative began under the name “Dustho Seba” (Service for the Needy). It started with a handful of individuals, limited resources, and a shared commitment to serve those most in need.

sections.title: From Humble Effort to Formal Foundation
paragraphs: Inspired by community-driven development and guided by the values of compassion and collective responsibility, this humble effort gradually grew through trust, consistency, and service.
After years of dedication and trust-building, the organization was formally registered in 2019 under the name Alokayon — marking the beginning of its structured journey.

sections.title: A Community-Powered Mission
paragraphs: What makes Alokayon unique is its foundation: it is non-political and non-profit, it receives no major institutional or foreign funding, and it is sustained by zakat, donations, and contributions from individuals.
Every contribution — no matter how small — is treated as a trust, to be delivered to those who need it most, with sincerity and accountability.

sections.title: From Small Efforts to Real Impact
paragraphs: Over the years, Alokayon has transformed compassion into meaningful action: supporting widows and unemployed individuals to become self-reliant, providing medical assistance to the elderly and disabled, funding education for underprivileged students, establishing schools for children in slum communities, distributing food, clothing, and emergency aid during crises, and offering interest-free financial support to help rebuild lives.
Each initiative is guided by one principle: not just to give, but to empower.

sections.title: A Vision Beyond Charity
paragraphs: Alokayon believes that charity is not merely about short-term relief — it is about restoring dignity, creating opportunity, and building a more just society.
It is about ensuring that a child continues their education, a widow regains independence, a family finds stability, and a community moves forward together.

sections.title: Lighting the Path Forward
paragraphs: Today, Alokayon stands as a growing movement of individuals united by faith, responsibility, and humanity.
What began as a small act of compassion has become a source of hope for many — and a reminder that when people come together with sincerity, even limited means can create lasting change.

caseStudy.title: A family's journey from crisis to stability
caseStudy.problem: Many families survive only on fragile daily earnings. When the main earner dies, the whole household can collapse overnight.
caseStudy.reality: Widowed mothers caring for orphaned children often become trapped in debt, losing the little stability they had left.
caseStudy.intervention: Alokayon identifies such families and responds with education support, direct financial help, and livelihood tools that reduce dependency.
caseStudy.impact: Children continue studying, families regain footing, and vulnerable households move from crisis toward dignity and stability.

hero.title: A free educational institution for underprivileged children
hero.description: Alokayon School was created to bring education, structure, and hope to children growing up in deprivation. More than fifty children receive free learning and educational materials.

items.label: Children admitted into government primary schools

whyMatters.title: Education can interrupt hardship early.
whyMatters.paragraphOne: Children carry the potential of tomorrow's teachers, leaders, artists, and thinkers. Yet many underprivileged children grow up without access to the food, care, and education needed to nurture that potential.
whyMatters.paragraphTwo: Alokayon School exists to interrupt that cycle early — so children are not left to drift into neglect, illiteracy, or harmful environments, but instead grow with discipline, dignity, and possibility.

founding.title: A small teaching effort became a shared mission.
founding.paragraphOne: For a long time, Alokayon wanted to create educational opportunities for underprivileged children but lacked the facilities and resources to begin.
founding.paragraphTwo: That changed when Jesmin Akter, who had been teaching slum children on the veranda of her house, came into contact with the organization. Alokayon responded by renting a small room, arranging seating, and providing books, notebooks, and pens. The school was then formally inaugurated as Alokayon Pathshala on 13 February 2023.

teacher.title: Meet the teacher behind the school
teacher.paragraphOne: Jesmin Akter's story gives this school its heart. After losing her father as a child, facing deep financial hardship, and enduring a difficult early marriage, she still held on to her dream of teaching.
teacher.paragraphTwo: She began by inviting neighborhood children to study, sometimes using unused pages from her sons' old notebooks. With Alokayon's support, that small effort grew into a proper school.
teacher.quote: With Alokayon's support, I now believe I can bring underprivileged children into the light of education, Insha'Allah.
teacher.name: Jesmin Akter

vision.title: A new model of madrasa education
vision.paragraphOne: This madrasa was established to move beyond a conventional system and introduce a modern, time-appropriate curriculum that integrates Arabic and Bengali education.
vision.paragraphTwo: The goal is not only to produce students with strong religious foundations, but also to equip them with the academic knowledge needed for the modern world.

leadership.title: Leadership built on sacrifice and integrity
leadership.description: The institution is led under the supervision of Mr. Abdul Matin (B.Sc.), Vice President of Alokayon. What makes this madrasa unique is not only its educational structure, but the sincerity and accountability at its core.
leadership.cardOneTitle: The director takes no salary or honorarium
leadership.cardTwoTitle: Personal pension support helps cover operational needs

hero.title: Real stories from the lives transformed through Alokayon
hero.description: From education to survival — these are not testimonials, they are journeys.

featuredStory.title: From struggle to stability
featuredStory.summary: Md. Nasim Uddin grew up in severe financial hardship, often struggling to afford both food and education. Despite academic excellence, continuing his studies in Dhaka remained uncertain.
featuredStory.summaryTwo: Through Alokayon's support — including tuition, books, exam fees, and family assistance — he was able to complete his higher education. Today, he gives back as a teacher serving the same community that once stood beside him.
fullStory: Nasim Uddin came from a lower middle-class family in a remote part of Jhenaidah, where continuing education required extraordinary sacrifice. He studied while working in the fields with his father and tutoring others whenever possible.
After moving to Dhaka for higher education, he faced painful uncertainty around mess rent, food, and academic costs. Even then, he kept trying to continue his studies with dignity.

Alokayon stepped in with support for tuition, books, exam fees, and broader family needs. That support expanded into medical help for his parents, repairs after storm damage, and interest-free assistance.
He later completed both Honors and Master's studies, sat for the 46th BCS preliminary examination, and now serves as a Senior Teacher at Alokayon Pathshala-1.
His story reflects what timely, thoughtful support can do when it reaches the right person at the right moment.
featuredStory.cardDescription: A journey shaped by education support, family care, and the chance to give back.

financial.description: Detailed financial statements are available in the annual magazine and downloadable records. These figures help visitors understand the scale of giving and distribution.

hero.title: Voices of trust from our donors
hero.description: Individuals across the world support Alokayon with confidence — guided by transparency, impact, and faith.

floatingCard.label: Donors from Bangladesh, India, and the United States

featured.title: Trust shaped through transparency
featured.quote: Observing the transparency and accountability in Alokayon's operations, I felt inspired to become associated with the organization.
featured.name: Professor Harunur Rashid Sikder
featured.role: Freedom Fighter, Former Chairman, Dhaka Education Board

items.quote: Collective effort creates greater opportunities to support the underprivileged, and Alokayon makes that effort meaningful.
items.name: Professor Dr. Md. Tohor Ali
items.role: Former Head, Department of Community Medicine, Sir Salimullah Medical College & Mitford Hospital

items.quote: I felt assured that my zakat and donations would be properly utilized through Alokayon and genuinely reach those in need.
items.name: Md. Abdul Momen

items.quote: Even from abroad, I continue supporting Alokayon financially because I trust its purpose, structure, and sincerity.
items.name: Md. Mohiuddin Babu

items.quote: Social service is not optional for those who are able; it is a responsibility, and Alokayon carries that responsibility with care.
items.name: Kamrul Hasan

globalSupport.title: A growing community of trust across borders
globalSupport.description: Alokayon's supporters include donors and well-wishers from Bangladesh, India, and the United States — people connected by trust in transparent, faith-aligned impact.

items.title: How I became associated with Alokayon
items.summary: A donor's relationship with Alokayon often begins with trust in the people behind the work and grows through seeing its sincerity in action.
paragraphs: Md. Mohiuddin Babu described learning about Alokayon directly from Fazlul Kader and choosing to become involved after understanding that it was a registered, non-political, non-profit organization built to support vulnerable people.

From the United States, he continues to contribute financially on a monthly basis while also offering guidance and advice, motivated by confidence in the organization's educational and poverty-alleviation work.
items.name: Md. Mohiuddin Babu

items.title: My coordination with Alokayon Charity
items.summary: Even from abroad, donors remain connected to Alokayon through regular communication, zakat, and yearly contributions.
paragraphs: Mumtaz Meher shared that her family remained in regular contact with Alokayon's founder and became convinced by the organization's service-oriented mission and formal government approval.
She and her children continue supporting Alokayon from North Carolina through zakat and donations, especially because of its work for helpless families and educational initiatives for slum children.
items.name: Mumtaz Meher

items.title: Trust from beyond Bangladesh
items.summary: Some donors support Alokayon from other countries because they trust both the organization and the people behind it.
paragraphs: Affan Abbasi explained that his trust in Alokayon was shaped by the reputation of its founders and the clarity of its work for widows, orphans, and underprivileged families.
He emphasized that in charitable giving, people first seek trust before offering their hard-earned income — and that this trust is what makes Alokayon stand out even from abroad.
items.name: Affan Abbasi

whyTrust.title: Trust grows when sincerity is backed by structure.
whyTrust.description: Across these donor reflections, the same themes appear again and again: transparency, zakat accountability, visible impact, and faith-driven service without political or commercial motives.
points: Transparent financial practices and visible accountability
Direct support to real beneficiaries in genuine hardship
Faith-aligned charitable work grounded in sincerity
No political or commercial agenda
Proven impact across education, healthcare, and relief

cta.title: Join a growing community of donors who believe in meaningful, transparent impact.
cta.description: Support work that is trusted by donors across countries and rooted in sincere service to vulnerable people.
